TEXT.source: NTU Corpus stories for Kavalan, story: KavNr-ancestor_buya

S.FORM: tangi pakunku pa iku tu zana masang baqita nizi tayilanan.
S.TRANSL: 今天我要講一個故事, 是有關我們祖先起源於宜蘭的故事。
Today, I am going to tell a story about our ancestors coming from Yi-lan.

S.FORM: masang tayan tayilanan nani mwaza zengnana mwaza banangna baqian tayan na baybedan bayita.
S.TRANSL: 從前在宜蘭有很多我們祖先的水田和土地。
Some time ago in Yi-lan there were many farms and lands of our ancestors’.

S.FORM: yau ti busus maseq taiyan matiw ta yilanan nani.
S.TRANSL: 然後就有漢人到了宜蘭去。
Then, Han people arrived there…went to Yi-lan.

S.FORM: supaR qaniawu kasianem tu sanaqunian tu mala tu zengnana baqita bayita.
S.TRANSL: 他們很懂得計畫該如何才能得到祖先們的水田。
They were good at planning how to obtain our ancestors’ farms.

S.FORM: wanay kebalan muaza lisinna tu zana metiwuay.
S.TRANSL: 由於噶瑪蘭人有很多的迷信, 比方說巫師之類的東西。
Since Kavalan people have many superstitions, such as something about witches.

S.FORM: zinna kasianem.
S.TRANSL: 他們如此認為。
They think so.

S.FORM: yau 'nay lasap lasapay Ray naRay Raybang taqa ti tu taiyan tu qayzuanan.
S.TRANSL: 某個地方有髒東西的時候, 他們就會放棄那裡。
When there is something dirty (unclean) in a place, they will abandon that place.

S.FORM: nayau supaR ti 'daq.
S.TRANSL: 別人知道了這件事。
Others knew it.

S.FORM: supaR ti busus tu waway na kebalan Ranaw nayawanna ti.
S.TRANSL: 漢人知道了噶瑪蘭的習俗, 所以他們就那樣。
The Han people knew the customs of Kavalan, so they did so.

S.FORM: titaanna baq na baqita na bayita nani.
S.TRANSL: 我們的祖先們就看到了(田裡死去的狗或雞)。
Our ancestors saw them (dead dogs or chickens in the farm).

S.FORM: izauanna ti tayan idadanna ti sukaw ti kowayan zau zinna baqian haw.
S.TRANSL: 他們把地留在那裡不管,並且把它圍起來,這個田就不能耕作了,老人們這麼想。
(They) left them there and surrounded (that place). The land cannot be plowed, thought the elders.

S.FORM: nani tenes mai ti niquayan ay titaanna na busus nani.
S.TRANSL: 那個很久沒有人耕作的田被漢人看到了。
The land which had not been plowed for a long time was seen by the Hans.

S.FORM: mai kowayannumi zau.
S.TRANSL: 你們很久沒有耕作這塊田了。
(You) haven’t plowed this land for a long time.

S.FORM: aiku pa ti zinna sadiwayanna ti nayau zengna na baqian taiyan nani.
S.TRANSL: “輪到我佔據(噶瑪蘭)祖先那邊的田地了”, 漢人這麼想。
It’s my turn, thought the Hans, to occupy the farms of the (Kavalan) ancestors there.

S.FORM: Runtu tabay pameng ti masiyun masiyun nani pameng ti kitut ti.
S.TRANSL: 土地本來很寬大,但不知不覺地漸漸變小了。
The land was originally wide and large, but became smaller gradually.

S.FORM: titaanna baqian taiyanan 'nay baq na baqita taiyan nani.
S.TRANSL: 我們的祖先看了之後。
Our ancestors there saw it.

S.FORM: mai ti ita tu zengna taziyan.
S.TRANSL: 我們這邊沒有田了。
We had no farms here.

S.FORM: samawmang pa ita taziyan ka.
S.TRANSL: 我們在這邊也沒用, 幹嘛留在這裡呢。
It’s useless for us to stay here.

S.FORM: qangima sayza matadin zinna nani matadin ti pasa tiboR.
S.TRANSL: 他們想說乾脆離開好了,(所以)他們就往南遷移。
They think they should leave (so) they left for the south.

S.FORM: mzaqis tu ipaz qabawa ay sumaqai ay nayaw ti mbuRaw pasa qalinku.
S.TRANSL: (他們)搭竹筏,搭船,或走路遷移往花蓮。
(They) left for Hualien by raft, boat, or on foot.

S.FORM: muna niqaqaanna maseq tu qalinku nani.
S.TRANSL: 他們首先到達的地方是花蓮。
The place where they arrived first is Hualien.

S.FORM: yau taizaan taqeliawanan maseq taqeliawanan.
S.TRANSL: 有到那個茄禮村, 到達茄禮村。
In qeliawanan.

S.FORM: wa tabay taziyan nengi qaizuwanan ta taziyan zinna qaniawu nani.
S.TRANSL: 他們說, ‘這裡很寬,是可以住的好地方。
‘It is so wide here and is thus a good place for living,’ said they.

S.FORM: taziyan ti qaniau kumoway.
S.TRANSL: 他們在這裡開墾了。
They plowed here.

S.FORM: neng tengnes nani yau ti zipun tu miza ay tu lazat tayan kuan ay tu iza nani.
S.TRANSL: 過了很久,有日本人來管理這些人。
After a long time, there (came) Japanese governing these people.

S.FORM: titaanna skaw sayza ni nikuananna nani.
S.TRANSL: 看了之後,(我們祖先)可能認為管理的方式不好。
After seeing this, (our ancestors) probably thought that how the Japanese governed the people was bad.”

S.FORM: miza ti masawa ti tu zipun.
S.TRANSL: 就跟日本人打。
They fought against the Japanese.

S.FORM: suqas kebalan atu qizaya' taiyan nani.
S.TRANSL: 噶瑪蘭和阿美族輸了。
Kavalan and Amis there lost.

S.FORM: maitis qakapaRanna zipun nani matalin ti uman.
S.TRANSL: 怕被日本人抓到, (他們)再換地方。
(They) are afraid of being caught by the Japanese so transferred to another place again.

S.FORM: sa pasazui ta qoRo'an tataqemuanan.
S.TRANSL: 往那個磯萁和龜庵。
To qoRo' and taqemuan.

S.FORM: yau tataqemuanan yau tasinsiaan yau ta a yau pateR tapateRungan.
S.TRANSL: 有到龜庵,有到新社。
To taqemuan and pateRung

S.FORM: yau sa kulis pasazui ti uman taqeladutan atu ta tachenggongan zinna haw.
S.TRANSL: 有到立德,往那個方向再到樟原和成功。
To kulis, qeladut and chenggong.

S.FORM: X aimi baqiniq yau tasinsiaan tumuqaz tayan.
S.TRANSL: 我們的祖先在新社那邊停下來上岸。
Our ancestors went ashore there in Sinshe.

S.FORM: smuRong titaanna naung 'nay tayan.
S.TRANSL: 上岸後, 看到那裡的山。
After landing, they saw the mountains there.

S.FORM: yau iRuR hoN yau naung mzaki tu lazing zinna Ranaw.
S.TRANSL: 有河,有山,又靠近海邊,他們說。
There are rivers and mountains and (the place) is near the sea, said they

S.FORM: aimi kebalan mlizaq tu sazanumay.
S.TRANSL: 我們噶瑪蘭人喜歡有水的(地方)。
We Kavalan people like the place with water.

S.FORM: yau zanum yau zanum smuRuz e zinna.
S.TRANSL: 他們說,有水我們可以生存。
With water, we can survive, said they.

S.FORM: Ranaw yau ti tasinsiaan.
S.TRANSL: 所以就在新社。
Thus, (they stayed) in Xinshe.

S.FORM: mzaki tu lazing mzaki tu naung mzaki tu iRuR nayaw nani.
S.TRANSL: 靠近海邊,靠近山,靠近河,那樣” 。
Near the sea, near the mountain, (and) near a river.

S.FORM: tayan ti niz sinsia ay 'nay tangi.
S.TRANSL: 現在新社的所有的人在那邊。
All the Xinshe people now were there.

S.FORM: tuzus tu tangi aw tengnes ti qaya tasaw.
S.TRANSL: 一直到現在,時間也很久了。
Till now it has been a long time.

S.FORM: nengi wawai taziyan tasinsiaan qenabinus na kebalan taziyan.
S.TRANSL: 噶瑪蘭在新社這裡的生活很好。
Life of Kavalan here in Xinshe is good.

S.FORM: tangi qamwaz ay lazat sinsia sayzi ta pateRungan.
S.TRANSL: 最多的人口是新社。
Xinshe has the most population.

S.FORM: zuma nani RaRaya ti sunis ngid tu laytanan wia uman sa taziyan tataipakan qelawqaway sunis.
S.TRANSL: 另外,小孩長大了想要賺錢,又再去到台北這裡工作。
In addition, the children had grown up and wanted to make money so they went here again to Taipei to work.

S.FORM: wantima baqian baibudan talepawan mlingi tu lepaw temita ay tu zengna o.
S.TRANSL: 只剩下老人家留守家裡與看守田地。
Only the elder stay there, watching houses and farms.

S.FORM: pilay ti imu baqian baibudan zinku.
S.TRANSL: 我說,老人家你們辛苦了。
Elders, thanks for your hard work, said I.

S.FORM: qawmang palamlames pa imi taziyan 'nay tataipakan qelawqawayay zinku.
S.TRANSL: 我們在台北這裡會認真工作。
We will work hard here in Taipei.

S.FORM: nayau ma sikawmaanku wanai.
S.TRANSL: 我說的就這樣.謝謝。
That’s what I said. Thanks.
